examples: The sense of smell, or olfaction, is powerful.
Significant advances have been made in the understanding of the biological and chemical nature of olfaction, but many fundamental questions have yet to be answered.
In the realm of olfaction, we must make do with descriptions and recollections.
Paired with illuminated labels that fade to white the moment you want to read them, this is more a show of prestidigitation than olfaction.
According to two studies presented to the International Symposium on Olfaction and Taste, held in San Francisco this week, that is exactly what happens.
